title: Stop! Look! Listen!
description: Imperatives — commands, instructions, recipes. Just the bare verb. Add DON'T for negatives.
summary_pt_br: Imperativo em inglês é apenas o VERBO BASE (sem sujeito). 'Stop!', 'Come here', 'Sit down'. Negativa: DON'T + verbo. 'Don't run'. Pedir educadamente: 'Please + imperativo' ou 'Could you + verbo'.

scenes.text_en:
Mr. Kumar called the class to attention.
'SIT DOWN. OPEN your books. LOOK at page 10.
PLEASE DON'T TALK now. DON'T RUN in the hall.'
No 'you'. Just the verb.
scenes.text_pt:
Mr. Kumar chamou a turma.
'SENTEM. ABRAM os livros. OLHEM a página 10.
POR FAVOR NÃO CONVERSEM agora. NÃO CORRAM no corredor.'
Sem 'you'. Só o verbo.

scenes.title: Rules — simple but strict
scenes.body_en: Imperative = base verb, no subject. 'Stop.' 'Sit down.' 'Call me.' Negative: DON'T + base. 'Don't worry.' To soften: add 'PLEASE'. 'Please sit down.' Or use 'COULD YOU…?' for polite requests — that's a question, not an imperative.
scenes.body_pt: Imperativo = verbo base, sem 'you'. Negativa: DON'T + verbo. Para suavizar: PLEASE. Pedidos educados: 'Could you + verbo'.

examples.en: Come here. Don't worry. Please wait.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Stop! Look! Listen!". Imperatives — commands, instructions, recipes. Just the bare verb. Add DON'T for negatives. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Stop! Look! Listen!". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Stop! Look! Listen!'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Stop! Look! Listen!".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
